title: [Bug]: Security label allow lists accept non-newline control characters
summary: Security label allow-list validation rejects newlines and NUL bytes but still accepts other ASCII control characters such as tab or delete.
observed: SecurityLabelProfileConfig accepts allowed_classifications containing tab characters.
expected: Security label allow lists should reject every ASCII control character.
failing_test: Add a unit test that expects control-character-bearing security label allow-list values to fail validation.
impact: Unsafe allow-list values can make policy review and matching harder to reason about.
delivery_semantics: No delivery behavior changes. The issue is rejected during configuration validation.
security: Allow-list values are policy inputs and should fail closed when malformed.
acceptance: Security label allow-list values reject all ASCII control characters.
The regression test covers a non-newline control character.
Release notes describe the policy validation hardening.
tests: Run focused security label config tests and full validation.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
